design.title: LIN ASIC Indexed Design Reference
design.headline: One indexed playbook for architecture, sizing, corners, and debug.
design.summary: Structured reference for the LIN mixed-signal ASIC so future designs can reuse the same architecture, sizing logic, operating-point targets, corner strategy, and debug order.
design.narrative: This reference keeps the whole LIN ASIC design flow in one place: the chip architecture, the analog and digital block intent, the sizing formulas, the DC operating points, the AC and PSRR checks, the corner-closure order, and the exact scripts used to reproduce the analysis. The goal is not only to explain this design, but to leave behind a template that can be reused for the next regulator, transceiver, or mixed-signal interface without starting from a blank page.
design.technology: BCD 180nm for target architecture, generic180 for current simulation framework
design.signoff_boundary: Current scripts use generic process and temperature scalers for early closure. Replace them with foundry PDK corner decks for final silicon signoff.

flow_summary: The design is organized as an indexed flow so architecture, implementation, verification, and debug knowledge are captured in the same order the design was closed.
analysis_summary: Each analog block is checked in the same sequence: DC operating point first, AC shape and loop strength second, PSRR third, transient and load movement fourth, then PVT closure. Digital blocks use reset-state inspection, functional stimulus, and control-path reachability instead of small-signal checks.
script_summary: These commands are the operational entry points for retrieving the methodology, generating the webpage, and running block-level checks across operating conditions.

architecture.summary: VBAT feeds the high-voltage LIN rail and the analog rail. The 3.3V analog rail feeds the bandgap and becomes the source for the 1.8V digital rail. Digital configuration arrives through SPI, lands in the register file, then fans into the control logic and LIN controller.
architecture.power_tree_summary: VBAT -> LDO_LIN -> 5.0V LIN rail; VBAT -> LDO_ANA -> 3.3V analog rail; 3.3V analog rail -> LDO_DIG -> 1.8V digital rail; Bandgap 1.2V -> all LDO references

domains.name: VDD_LIN
domains.drives: lin_transceiver analog bus driver and wake path

domains.name: VDD_ANA
domains.drives: bandgap_ref, analog bias, I/O-facing analog circuitry

power_sequence: Enable and settle the bandgap before allowing any regulator loop to close.
Bring up the 3.3V analog LDO next so the bandgap and analog bias always have a clean rail.
Bring up the 1.8V digital LDO only after the 3.3V rail is valid, so digital logic never sees an undefined parent rail.
Enable the 5V LIN LDO and the LIN transceiver last, because the bus interface is the noisiest block and should not corrupt reference startup.

control_path.title: SPI programming path
control_path.description: SPI slave decodes host writes and forwards register transactions into the register file.

control_path.title: Register decode path
control_path.description: Register bits map into bandgap enable, LDO enables, sleep mode, LIN mode, and baud divider settings.

control_path.title: Sequencing path
control_path.description: Control logic staggers enable assertions and POR release so the analog and digital rails appear in a safe order.

indexed_flow.title: Freeze the chip architecture
indexed_flow.description: Lock the power domains, the control path, the LIN-facing external interfaces, and the ownership of each rail before sizing any transistor.

indexed_flow.title: Close the reference path first
indexed_flow.description: Design the bandgap and define the 1.2V reference target because every downstream regulator inherits its accuracy, startup behavior, and PSRR from this block.

indexed_flow.title: Size each regulator from the required rail
indexed_flow.description: Use the output target, dropout target, max load current, and stability requirement to size the divider, OTA, pass device, and compensation for each LDO.

indexed_flow.title: Close the physical interface
indexed_flow.description: Design the LIN transceiver bus thresholds, slew control, and pull-up network after the rails are defined so the analog PHY can be referenced to the right supplies and thresholds.

indexed_flow.title: Close the digital control plane
indexed_flow.description: Implement the SPI access path, register file, LIN protocol state machine, and the sequencer so software-visible control matches the analog bring-up assumptions.

indexed_flow.title: Capture closure and debug knowledge
indexed_flow.description: Record formulas, node targets, corner risks, pass criteria, and tuning knobs so future designs can reuse the same playbook.

analysis_playbook.name: DC operating point
analysis_playbook.purpose: Verify that every analog node biases where the architecture expects before asking the loop to deliver gain or rejection.
analysis_playbook.pass_criteria: Reference and feedback nodes bias correctly, pass device stays in the intended region, and the rail lands inside the nominal window.
analysis_playbook.debug_focus: If DC is wrong, fix reference level, divider ratio, bias current, or pass-device headroom before running AC or PSRR.

analysis_playbook.name: AC small-signal inspection
analysis_playbook.purpose: Inspect loop strength, pole/zero placement, and output-node behavior across frequency before running large-signal transients.
analysis_playbook.pass_criteria: Gain shape and dominant-pole placement support the intended phase margin once the output capacitor and compensation network are included.
analysis_playbook.debug_focus: If AC rolls off too early, increase OTA gm or reduce excess loading. If peaking appears, revisit compensation capacitor, ESR zero, or pass-gate pole placement.

analysis_playbook.name: PSRR extraction
analysis_playbook.purpose: Inject a 1V AC ripple at the supply source and compute how much reaches the regulated output or reference node.
analysis_playbook.pass_criteria: Meet the target dB rejection at low frequency and keep the roll-off understandable as the loop bandwidth is crossed.
analysis_playbook.debug_focus: If PSRR fails at low frequency, raise loop gain or bias accuracy. If it fails at high frequency, improve local decoupling, compensation, or the feedthrough path through the pass device.

analysis_playbook.name: Corner closure sweep
analysis_playbook.purpose: Repeat the analysis on TT, FF, SS, FS, and SF plus cold, room, and hot temperature points so the same block is checked against worst-case speed, threshold, and resistor drift.
analysis_playbook.pass_criteria: All required specs stay inside guard bands at every corner or the failing corner is tied to one explicit tuning knob and closed deliberately.
analysis_playbook.debug_focus: Never tune multiple knobs blindly. Note the failing corner, change one dominant knob, rerun, and record why the change worked.

corner_closure.summary: The closure flow starts with reference accuracy, then regulator headroom and stability, then bus threshold integrity, and finally the digital control path. The same matrix is reused for every future block so failures are easy to compare across designs.

matrix.voltage: VIN min / nominal / max according to each block domain

closure_order: Close the bandgap over temperature before trusting any LDO output target.
Close each LDO at minimum input headroom and maximum load current before optimizing nominal behavior.
Check AC and PSRR after the DC operating point is correct, not before.
Run transceiver dominant and recessive thresholds across VBAT min and max after the 5V rail is stable.
Run digital reset and control-path checks after the rail sequence is frozen so firmware-visible behavior matches power reality.
corner_closure.pass_rule: A block is considered closed only when the required spec stays inside limits across the full matrix or when a tighter implementation assumption is written down explicitly and becomes part of the architecture contract.
corner_closure.model_note: The current framework corner mode scales generic model parameters to expose sensitivity early. Final signoff must switch to foundry corner libraries and extracted parasitics.

script_index.name: Query the full design summary
script_index.purpose: Read the indexed manifest without opening the webpage.

script_index.name: List all recorded blocks
script_index.purpose: See which blocks have structured methodology entries.

script_index.name: Inspect one block formulas
script_index.purpose: Fetch only the sizing formulas for a target block.

script_index.name: Generate the webpage
script_index.purpose: Render the JSON manifest into a standalone HTML page for review and reuse.

script_index.name: Run DC on one block
script_index.purpose: Check node bias and output target under one operating corner.

script_index.name: Run AC on one block
script_index.purpose: Inspect small-signal behavior around the intended loop bandwidth.

script_index.name: Run PSRR on one block
script_index.purpose: Inject supply ripple and calculate rejection in dB.

debug_playbook.title: If DC fails first
debug_playbook.description: Fix reference voltage, bias current, divider ratio, or headroom before trying transient, AC, or PSRR tuning.

debug_playbook.title: If only hot corners fail
debug_playbook.description: Look for lost gm, excessive resistor drift, or pass-device overdrive collapse before widening devices indiscriminately.

debug_playbook.title: If only cold fast corners fail
debug_playbook.description: Look for too much loop bandwidth, reduced phase margin, or threshold shifts that overdrive the pass path.

debug_playbook.title: If PSRR fails but DC is correct
debug_playbook.description: Separate low-frequency loop-gain problems from high-frequency feedthrough problems so the wrong knob is not adjusted.

debug_playbook.title: If digital control disagrees with analog expectations
debug_playbook.description: Check reset defaults, register mapping, and enable sequencing before assuming the analog block is wrong.

future_design_reuse.title: Copy the manifest structure
future_design_reuse.description: Start the next design with the same top-level sections and only replace the block list, formulas, and file paths.

future_design_reuse.title: Keep one tuning knob per failure
future_design_reuse.description: When a corner fails, record the one parameter that moved the failure back into spec so the next design inherits the lesson.

future_design_reuse.title: Keep the webpage generated
future_design_reuse.description: The JSON file is the source of truth, but the HTML page is the fastest way for designers and reviewers to debug together.

blocks.name: Bandgap Reference
blocks.role: Generates the 1.2V reference that sets every downstream LDO output target and startup ordering decision.
blocks.architecture: Brokaw-style bandgap with a startup assist, a cascode-like PMOS mirror, an area-ratio BJT pair, emitter resistors for delta-VBE generation, and a PMOS output buffer.

implementation_steps.title: Create a guaranteed startup path
implementation_steps.description: The startup transistors inject current into the bias node so the bandgap does not sit in the zero-current metastable state.

implementation_steps.title: Generate PTAT using delta-VBE
implementation_steps.description: Q1 and Q2 use an 8:1 area ratio so delta-VBE is proportional to thermal voltage and rises with temperature.

implementation_steps.title: Sum PTAT and CTAT
implementation_steps.description: VBE provides the CTAT term and the resistor-scaled delta-VBE provides the PTAT term so the sum lands near zero tempco around room temperature.

primary_specs.name: VREF nominal
primary_specs.reason: Drives all LDO dividers and bias targets.

primary_specs.name: Tempco
primary_specs.reason: Prevents every rail from drifting with temperature.

primary_specs.name: PSRR
primary_specs.reason: Reference ripple directly appears on every regulated output if this fails.

primary_specs.name: Supply current
primary_specs.reason: Keeps always-on bias cost low.

sizing_formulas.title: PTAT voltage
sizing_formulas.formula: DeltaVBE = VT * ln(N), where N = emitter area ratio = 8
sizing_formulas.explanation: This sets the slope of the temperature-proportional term.

sizing_formulas.title: Bandgap sum
sizing_formulas.explanation: Choose the resistor ratio so the PTAT and CTAT slopes cancel near the desired operating temperature.

sizing_formulas.title: Branch current
sizing_formulas.explanation: Emitter resistor value sets the current level and therefore the mirror and buffer bias conditions.

dc_operating_points.how_set: PTAT plus CTAT sum through Q1/Q2 and the resistor network
dc_operating_points.why: This is the master reference for all regulators.

dc_operating_points.how_set: Startup path pushes the mirror away from the zero-current state
dc_operating_points.why: Without this node settling, the bandgap can latch into the off state.

dc_operating_points.how_set: Emitter resistors and BJT area ratio enforce delta-VBE
dc_operating_points.why: Defines the PTAT term.

analysis_steps.name: Reference bias check
analysis_steps.purpose: Confirm startup leaves the zero-current state and the reference settles around 1.2V.
analysis_steps.pass_criteria: VREF stays inside 1.14V to 1.26V and the mirror bias is non-zero.
analysis_steps.debug_focus: Startup strength, mirror bias resistance, resistor ratio R3/R2.

analysis_steps.name: Reference stiffness
analysis_steps.purpose: Inspect how much the output node moves with small-signal perturbation at the supply.
analysis_steps.pass_criteria: Output pole and buffer loading stay well controlled over the reference bandwidth.
analysis_steps.debug_focus: Output buffer size, output filter capacitor, mirror resistance.

analysis_steps.name: Bandgap PSRR
analysis_steps.purpose: Inject AC on VDD to see how much supply ripple leaks into VREF.
analysis_steps.pass_criteria: Low-frequency rejection meets or exceeds 60 dB where the regulators rely on reference quietness.
analysis_steps.debug_focus: Mirror ro, buffer feedthrough, output filtering.

corner_focus.risk: Low delta-VBE current and loss of startup margin
Too much current and excess output drift

tuning_knobs.title: R3 / R2 ratio
tuning_knobs.description: Moves the absolute VREF target and the PTAT-to-CTAT cancellation balance.

tuning_knobs.title: Startup transistor strength
tuning_knobs.description: Improves cold-start certainty but should not corrupt settled bias.

tuning_knobs.title: Mirror device length
tuning_knobs.description: Raises output resistance and improves PSRR at the cost of headroom.

blocks.name: LDO Analog 3.3V
blocks.role: Generates the 3.3V analog rail from VBAT and feeds the bandgap, analog biasing, and the parent rail for the digital regulator.
blocks.architecture: Folded-cascode OTA compares VREF against the feedback node, drives a large PMOS pass device, and stabilizes the closed loop with Miller compensation plus an output capacitor and ESR zero.

implementation_steps.title: Set divider ratio from the 1.2V reference
implementation_steps.description: The feedback divider is chosen first so the OTA always regulates to the right closed-loop output.

implementation_steps.title: Size the pass PMOS from load and dropout
implementation_steps.description: Pass width is chosen to hold the worst-case current while keeping Vov small enough to meet dropout.

implementation_steps.title: Shape the loop with compensation
implementation_steps.description: C_COMP, R_COMP, C_COMP2, and the output capacitor set the dominant pole, pole splitting, and ESR zero placement.

primary_specs.name: Output
primary_specs.reason: Feeds bandgap, analog I/O, and the digital LDO input.

primary_specs.name: Max load
primary_specs.reason: Covers analog bias plus downstream rail demand.

primary_specs.name: Dropout
primary_specs.reason: Maintains regulation near VBAT minimum.

primary_specs.name: PSRR
primary_specs.reason: Prevents battery ripple from polluting the analog rail.

sizing_formulas.title: Closed-loop output target
sizing_formulas.explanation: With VREF = 1.2V and VOUT = 3.3V, R1/R2 ~= 1.75.

sizing_formulas.title: Pass-device minimum width
sizing_formulas.explanation: Choose the PMOS width so the pass device can source worst-case load at the allowed overdrive and dropout.

sizing_formulas.title: Pass-device transconductance
sizing_formulas.explanation: This sets the loop gain and the output pole interaction.

sizing_formulas.title: Loop gain estimate
sizing_formulas.explanation: Use this to see whether low-frequency regulation and PSRR are plausible before running full AC.

sizing_formulas.title: Dominant output pole
sizing_formulas.explanation: This pole must be positioned with compensation so phase margin remains healthy across load.

sizing_formulas.title: PSRR extraction
sizing_formulas.explanation: The framework uses a 1V AC ripple on the supply source, so the output ripple directly maps into PSRR.

dc_operating_points.how_set: Feedback divider forces n_fb to match the 1.2V reference
dc_operating_points.why: Main supply target for all analog circuitry.

dc_operating_points.how_set: R_FB1 and R_FB2 divide VOUT down to the reference
dc_operating_points.why: The OTA regulates around this equality point.

dc_operating_points.how_set: R_BIAS and M_BIAS create the operating current for the OTA
dc_operating_points.why: If this node moves, gm and phase margin move with it.

dc_operating_points.target: low enough below VIN to source max load
dc_operating_points.how_set: OTA output drives the PMOS gate until VOUT closes the loop
dc_operating_points.why: Determines dropout and load regulation.

analysis_steps.name: Nominal DC regulation
analysis_steps.purpose: Check the divider ratio, OTA bias, and the steady-state rail.
analysis_steps.pass_criteria: VOUT inside 3.1V to 3.5V and feedback node near 1.2V.
analysis_steps.debug_focus: Divider ratio, pass width, bias resistor.

analysis_steps.name: Small-signal stability shape
analysis_steps.purpose: Inspect how the loop rolls off before transient load tests are trusted.
analysis_steps.pass_criteria: No aggressive peaking near the unity-gain region and a controllable pole/zero sequence.
analysis_steps.debug_focus: C_COMP, R_COMP, output capacitor ESR, OTA gm.

analysis_steps.name: Supply ripple rejection
analysis_steps.purpose: Inject battery ripple and measure how much reaches the 3.3V rail.
analysis_steps.pass_criteria: At least 50 dB rejection at 1 kHz and understandable degradation above loop bandwidth.
analysis_steps.debug_focus: OTA gain, pass-device feedthrough, local decoupling.

analysis_steps.name: PVT rail closure
analysis_steps.purpose: Run TT, FF, SS, FS, and SF across cold, room, and hot for the same checks.
analysis_steps.pass_criteria: Output remains inside the rail window and the bias nodes remain valid under the worst headroom case.
analysis_steps.debug_focus: Pass width first, OTA bias second, compensation third.

corner_focus.condition: SS hot with low VIN headroom
corner_focus.risk: Pass device loses gm and dropout rises
corner_focus.primary_knob: Increase M_PASS width or reduce VOV target

corner_focus.risk: Loop bandwidth can run too fast and phase margin shrinks
corner_focus.primary_knob: Increase compensation or reduce excess OTA gm

tuning_knobs.title: M_PASS width
tuning_knobs.description: Primary knob for dropout, DC regulation under load, and pass-device gm.

tuning_knobs.title: R_FB1 / R_FB2
tuning_knobs.description: Moves the absolute output target; use only after the reference is trusted.

tuning_knobs.title: C_COMP / R_COMP
tuning_knobs.description: Primary knobs for phase-margin cleanup once DC is already correct.

tuning_knobs.title: R_BIAS and OTA sizing
tuning_knobs.description: Moves OTA gm and therefore low-frequency loop gain and PSRR.

blocks.name: LDO Digital 1.8V
blocks.role: Generates the 1.8V core rail from the 3.3V analog rail and is tuned for digital load movement.
blocks.architecture: Two-stage error amplifier with a PMOS pass device, large output decoupling, a 1.8V divider, and simpler compensation than the 3.3V analog LDO.

implementation_steps.title: Derive the divider from the same 1.2V reference
implementation_steps.description: For 1.8V output, the divider uses a 0.5 ratio so the feedback node still lands at 1.2V.

implementation_steps.title: Bias for digital load steps
implementation_steps.description: Output capacitance is made intentionally large to absorb fast core transients while the loop catches up.

implementation_steps.title: Keep the loop simple
implementation_steps.description: This regulator favors robust transient behavior and easy closure over elaborate high-gain analog shaping.

primary_specs.name: Output
primary_specs.reason: Digital core rail for RTL blocks.

primary_specs.name: Max load
primary_specs.reason: Matches controller, register, and interface demand.

primary_specs.name: Dropout
primary_specs.reason: Keeps the 1.8V domain alive as the 3.3V rail moves.

primary_specs.name: Transient robustness
primary_specs.reason: Digital load steps are sharper than analog bias movement.

sizing_formulas.title: Divider target
sizing_formulas.formula: VOUT = 1.2V * (1 + R1 / R2) -> choose R1 = 50k, R2 = 100k
sizing_formulas.explanation: The divider is intentionally simple so the digital core target is easy to audit.

sizing_formulas.title: Pass width sizing
sizing_formulas.explanation: Same pass-device rule as the analog LDO, but tuned for lower output voltage and lower max load.

sizing_formulas.title: Output pole
sizing_formulas.explanation: Large output capacitance pushes the output pole low enough to tame digital load shocks.

sizing_formulas.title: PSRR extraction
sizing_formulas.explanation: Use the same injection method as the analog LDO and compare the 1.8V output ripple directly.

dc_operating_points.how_set: Divider and OTA close the loop around 1.2V feedback
dc_operating_points.why: Defines digital timing and logic swing margins.

dc_operating_points.how_set: 50k / 100k divider ratio from VOUT to ground
dc_operating_points.why: Direct loop comparison node.

dc_operating_points.target: OTA bias established from VIN through R_BIAS
dc_operating_points.how_set: Bias transistor and resistor create a stable tail current
dc_operating_points.why: Sets gain and dynamic response.

analysis_steps.name: Digital rail target
analysis_steps.purpose: Confirm the rail lands near 1.8V and the divider biases properly.
analysis_steps.pass_criteria: VOUT inside 1.7V to 1.9V.
analysis_steps.debug_focus: Divider ratio, pass width, bias strength.

analysis_steps.name: Digital-rail loop shape
analysis_steps.purpose: Make sure the larger output capacitor does not hide an unstable loop.
analysis_steps.pass_criteria: Controlled response without large peaking near the crossover region.
analysis_steps.debug_focus: C_COMP, C_OUT, OTA gm.

analysis_steps.name: Core-rail PSRR
analysis_steps.purpose: Measure how much 3.3V rail ripple reaches the 1.8V core.
analysis_steps.pass_criteria: Enough rejection that digital noise and parent-rail noise do not collapse timing margin.
analysis_steps.debug_focus: OTA gain and output decoupling.

corner_focus.risk: Very fast loop with reduced phase margin under light load
Loss of load regulation under peak current

tuning_knobs.title: C_OUT
tuning_knobs.description: First knob for digital load-step containment.

tuning_knobs.title: C_COMP
tuning_knobs.description: Primary phase-margin knob when the output pole shifts with load.

tuning_knobs.title: M_PASS width
tuning_knobs.description: Restores dropout and DC accuracy at slow hot corners.

blocks.name: LDO LIN 5.0V
blocks.role: Generates the 5V LIN transceiver rail from VBAT and must tolerate the widest supply span and the strongest bus-driver disturbances.
blocks.architecture: High-voltage PMOS pass device with a VBAT-capable input stage, divider set for 5V, explicit output capacitor, and compensation chosen for a bus-driver load rather than a quiet bias rail.

implementation_steps.title: Use an HV pass path
implementation_steps.description: The pass transistor and its input-facing devices must tolerate 8V to 18V without losing margin.

implementation_steps.title: Set the 5V divider
implementation_steps.description: With 1.2V reference, the divider ratio is roughly 3.167 to reach the LIN rail target.

implementation_steps.title: Close against the noisiest consumer
implementation_steps.description: The regulator is sized and compensated for the LIN transceiver, which is more impulsive than the bandgap or digital core.

primary_specs.name: Output
primary_specs.reason: Primary supply for the LIN physical layer.

primary_specs.name: VIN range
primary_specs.reason: Battery / automotive supply spread.

primary_specs.name: Dropout
primary_specs.reason: Maintains line-driver margin near low battery.

primary_specs.name: Max load
primary_specs.reason: Covers dominant-state driver surges and interface biasing.

sizing_formulas.title: 5V divider ratio
sizing_formulas.explanation: This ratio is implemented by 316.7k over 100k in the current netlist.

sizing_formulas.title: HV pass-device sizing
sizing_formulas.explanation: The high-voltage PMOS has lower effective transconductance than the core device, so width must grow accordingly.

sizing_formulas.title: PSRR extraction
sizing_formulas.explanation: Supply ripple on VBAT should not become logic threshold movement in the LIN receiver through the 5V rail.

dc_operating_points.how_set: OTA and HV pass device regulate until the divider feedback equals VREF
dc_operating_points.why: Defines the analog PHY operating headroom.

dc_operating_points.how_set: 316.7k / 100k divider from the 5V rail
dc_operating_points.why: Direct loop comparison node.

dc_operating_points.how_set: High-value bias resistor from VIN establishes the OTA current without excess power burn
dc_operating_points.why: Prevents hot-battery overbias.

analysis_steps.name: 5V rail regulation
analysis_steps.purpose: Check the rail at nominal and worst-case battery headroom.
analysis_steps.pass_criteria: VOUT inside 4.75V to 5.25V.
analysis_steps.debug_focus: HV pass-device width and divider ratio.

analysis_steps.name: 5V loop shape
analysis_steps.purpose: Inspect the pole movement caused by the bus-facing load and the 2.2u output capacitor.
analysis_steps.pass_criteria: Controlled roll-off without unstable peaking.
analysis_steps.debug_focus: C_COMP, R_COMP, output-cap sizing.

analysis_steps.name: VBAT ripple rejection
analysis_steps.purpose: Measure how much battery ripple leaks into the 5V transceiver rail.
analysis_steps.pass_criteria: Rejection is high enough that receiver thresholds and bus-drive behavior remain stable.
analysis_steps.debug_focus: Low-frequency loop gain and direct pass-device feedthrough.

corner_focus.risk: Largest dropout and weakest pass path
Loop can become too aggressive and overshoot the 5V target

tuning_knobs.title: HV M_PASS width
tuning_knobs.description: Primary control of low-battery regulation and dominant-state current reserve.

tuning_knobs.title: R_COMP plus compensation caps
tuning_knobs.description: Phase-margin correction when the transceiver load changes the pole map.

tuning_knobs.title: Bias resistor
tuning_knobs.description: Balances OTA current against VBAT power loss.

blocks.name: LIN Transceiver
blocks.role: Implements the single-wire physical layer: dominant pull-down, recessive pull-up, slew control, hysteretic receive path, sleep gating, and wake detection.
blocks.architecture: Open-drain dominant driver with RC-shaped predriver, VBAT pull-up plus diode, resistor-generated receiver thresholds around 0.4 and 0.6 of VBAT, and positive feedback for hysteresis.

implementation_steps.title: Set bus thresholds from VBAT
implementation_steps.description: The resistor ladder creates nominal thresholds near 0.4 * VBAT and 0.6 * VBAT for dominant and recessive decisions.

implementation_steps.title: Limit dV/dt for EMC
implementation_steps.description: The predriver RC and gate-shaping devices slow the large bus transistor enough to land inside LIN slew expectations.

implementation_steps.title: Gate the analog path in sleep
implementation_steps.description: Sleep-mode devices shut off TX and RX bias paths to save current while the wake detector keeps monitoring the bus.

primary_specs.name: Dominant bus level
primary_specs.reason: Meets LIN physical-layer low-state requirement.

primary_specs.name: Recessive bus level
primary_specs.reason: Meets LIN physical-layer high-state requirement.

primary_specs.name: Slew rate
primary_specs.reason: EMC and bus-shape compliance.

primary_specs.name: Sleep current
primary_specs.reason: Automotive standby power.

sizing_formulas.title: Receiver thresholds
sizing_formulas.explanation: The divider and hysteresis network are chosen around the LIN receive windows.

sizing_formulas.title: Driver strength
sizing_formulas.explanation: Use the dominant-state current target to size the high-voltage NMOS bus device.

sizing_formulas.title: Slew approximation
sizing_formulas.explanation: The RC predriver deliberately limits gate current so the bus edge stays controlled.

dc_operating_points.how_set: R_PU and diode pull the bus high when the driver is off
dc_operating_points.why: Defines logic 1 on the bus.

dc_operating_points.how_set: High-voltage NMOS pulls the bus low in dominant state
dc_operating_points.why: Defines logic 0 on the bus.

dc_operating_points.how_set: Threshold resistor ladder from VBAT to ground
dc_operating_points.why: Sets receiver decisions and hysteresis center.

dc_operating_points.target: receiver bias active only when not in sleep
dc_operating_points.how_set: Bias resistor and sleep gating transistors
dc_operating_points.why: Cuts standby current while preserving wake detection.

analysis_steps.name: Static bus states
analysis_steps.purpose: Check dominant and recessive levels against LIN expectations.
analysis_steps.pass_criteria: Bus high above 10V nominal and bus low below 2V nominal.
analysis_steps.debug_focus: M_DRV size, pull-up network, diode drop.

analysis_steps.name: Slew and wake behavior
analysis_steps.purpose: Observe bus transition shape and sleep-to-wake behavior in time domain.
analysis_steps.pass_criteria: Edges stay controlled and sleep gating behaves predictably.
analysis_steps.debug_focus: R_SR1, C_SR, wake detector path.

analysis_steps.name: Threshold robustness
analysis_steps.purpose: Check the threshold ladder and hysteresis path across VBAT, process, and temperature movement.
analysis_steps.pass_criteria: Threshold spacing and bus voltage windows still map to dominant/recessive decisions.
analysis_steps.debug_focus: Threshold resistors, hysteresis transistor, receiver bias.

corner_focus.risk: Edge rate becomes too sharp and thresholds shift

tuning_knobs.title: M_DRV width
tuning_knobs.description: Primary dominant-level and current-drive knob.

tuning_knobs.title: R_SR1 / C_SR
tuning_knobs.description: Primary EMC and edge-rate knob.

tuning_knobs.title: Threshold ladder
tuning_knobs.description: Moves receiver high and low decision levels.

tuning_knobs.title: M_HYS
tuning_knobs.description: Controls hysteresis window width and noise immunity.

files.role: Top-level integration anchor for the transceiver

blocks.name: SPI Slave Controller
blocks.role: Provides the software-visible write and read entry point into the chip configuration and status registers.
blocks.architecture: Mode-0 SPI slave with synchronized SCLK and CS_N edges, a 16-bit frame, separate shift-in and shift-out paths, and decoded register read/write strobes.

implementation_steps.title: Synchronize external edges
implementation_steps.description: SCLK and CS_N are synchronized into the core clock domain before decode so the rest of the logic sees clean events.

implementation_steps.title: Decode the 16-bit frame
implementation_steps.description: The first byte carries R/W plus address, and the second byte carries write data or readback payload timing.

implementation_steps.title: Isolate read and write strobes
implementation_steps.description: reg_rd and reg_wr pulse only at the intended frame boundaries so the register file stays deterministic.

primary_specs.name: Protocol
primary_specs.reason: Host interface assumption.

primary_specs.name: Frame
primary_specs.reason: 1 R/W bit plus 7-bit address plus 8-bit payload.

primary_specs.name: Outputs
primary_specs.reason: These signals drive the full digital control plane.

sizing_formulas.title: Transaction length
sizing_formulas.explanation: Use this to size firmware access bandwidth and verify decode timing budget.

sizing_formulas.title: Sampling margin
sizing_formulas.formula: fSCLK should stay well below the core clock edge-detect rate used by the synchronizers
sizing_formulas.explanation: The synchronizer depth and core clock determine the safe SPI envelope.

dc_operating_points.how_set: External host keeps chip-select high between transactions
dc_operating_points.why: Frame counter and strobes remain idle.

dc_operating_points.how_set: CS_N falling edge resets the counter and CS_N rising edge terminates the transfer
dc_operating_points.why: Avoids partial-frame decode.

dc_operating_points.how_set: Decode only at the 8-bit and 16-bit boundaries
dc_operating_points.why: Protects the register file from repeated writes.

analysis_steps.name: Frame decode
analysis_steps.purpose: Check that address and data land on the expected outputs.
analysis_steps.pass_criteria: DIG-SPI-DECODE passes and the decoded address/control handoff matches the frame.
analysis_steps.debug_focus: Edge synchronizers, bit counter, RW decode.

corner_focus.condition: Fast external SCLK relative to core clock
corner_focus.risk: Edge detector misses or double-counts transitions
corner_focus.primary_knob: Synchronizer strategy and allowed SCLK ratio

tuning_knobs.title: Synchronizer depth
tuning_knobs.description: Improves CDC robustness if the host clock approaches the local clock domain too closely.

tuning_knobs.title: Frame decode boundaries
tuning_knobs.description: Useful when debugging off-by-one address or payload handoff errors.

blocks.name: Register File
blocks.role: Stores configuration and status fields for enables, sleep mode, baud divider, payload staging, and IRQ masking.
blocks.architecture: Addressed register bank with reset defaults, write decode, combinational read mux, and decoded control outputs that directly feed the analog and protocol control paths.

implementation_steps.title: Define reset defaults
implementation_steps.description: The reset image intentionally enables the base power mask so the design boots into a known state.

implementation_steps.title: Keep control decode transparent
implementation_steps.description: Control bits fan out directly into bgr_en and the LDO enables so the relationship between software and hardware is easy to debug.

implementation_steps.title: Separate status from control
implementation_steps.description: Status fields are updated from runtime observations while control fields remain software-owned.

primary_specs.name: CTRL reset value
primary_specs.reason: Boots with base power enables asserted.

primary_specs.name: LIN_CTRL reset value
primary_specs.reason: Default LIN enable state captured in RTL.

primary_specs.name: Baud divider
primary_specs.reason: Feeds the LIN controller bit timing.

sizing_formulas.title: Baud divider combine
sizing_formulas.explanation: This is the exact register-to-timing mapping used by the RTL.

sizing_formulas.title: Power mask decode
sizing_formulas.explanation: The default mask and software writes directly define the analog bring-up behavior.

dc_operating_points.how_set: Reset branch of the sequential block
dc_operating_points.why: Defines the power-on default enable mask.

dc_operating_points.how_set: CTRL bit 4 decode
dc_operating_points.why: Controls the sequencer and LIN enable path.

dc_operating_points.how_set: Concatenate high and low divider bytes
dc_operating_points.why: Feeds the protocol timing engine.

analysis_steps.name: Reset and write path
analysis_steps.purpose: Check defaults, write behavior, and fanout into the enable outputs.
analysis_steps.pass_criteria: DIG-REGFILE-CONTROL passes with the expected default mask and baud divider writeback.
analysis_steps.debug_focus: Address decode, sequential write block, combinational output mapping.

corner_focus.risk: Software-visible defaults do not match hardware expectation
corner_focus.primary_knob: Reset timing and register initialization order

tuning_knobs.title: Reset values
tuning_knobs.description: First place to fix any mismatch between intended startup order and observed control outputs.

tuning_knobs.title: Register map addresses
tuning_knobs.description: Use when software and hardware disagree on which field is being touched.

blocks.name: LIN Protocol Controller
blocks.role: Implements LIN protocol framing, break handling, sync byte handling, PID parity, checksum calculation, UART shifting, and IRQ generation.
blocks.architecture: State machine wrapped around UART TX/RX primitives with break detection, PID generation, enhanced checksum logic, and master or slave behavior selected by control registers.

implementation_steps.title: Generate a baud tick from baud_div
implementation_steps.description: The controller converts the programmed divider into the timing base for TX, RX, and break detection.

implementation_steps.title: Map protocol phases to explicit states
implementation_steps.description: Break, sync, PID, data, checksum, sleep, wake, and error states are all separated so protocol reachability can be checked directly.

implementation_steps.title: Keep parity and checksum local
implementation_steps.description: The PID and checksum logic stay inside the controller so framing bugs are isolated from the register path.

primary_specs.name: Break detection
primary_specs.reason: Core LIN framing requirement.

primary_specs.name: Sync byte
primary_specs.reason: Core LIN framing requirement.

primary_specs.name: PID parity
primary_specs.reason: Protocol integrity.

primary_specs.name: Checksum
primary_specs.reason: Protocol compatibility.

sizing_formulas.title: Baud tick
sizing_formulas.explanation: This is the exact timing primitive used by the controller.

sizing_formulas.title: PID parity bits
sizing_formulas.formula: P0 = ID0 xor ID1 xor ID2 xor ID4; P1 = ~(ID1 xor ID3 xor ID4 xor ID5)
sizing_formulas.explanation: These parity equations are implemented directly in the RTL function.

sizing_formulas.title: Enhanced checksum
sizing_formulas.formula: checksum = bitwise_not(sum(PID + data bytes with carry wrap-around))
sizing_formulas.explanation: Carry wrap-around preserves LIN checksum semantics.

dc_operating_points.how_set: Sequential reset block initializes state and key outputs
dc_operating_points.why: Makes the protocol path deterministic at startup.

dc_operating_points.how_set: UART transmitter defaults txd to 1 when not busy
dc_operating_points.why: Matches LIN bus idle behavior.

dc_operating_points.how_set: IRQ is OR reduction of flags and enables
dc_operating_points.why: Prevents false interrupts.

analysis_steps.name: State-machine readiness
analysis_steps.purpose: Check reset, master/slave readiness, and key control-path behavior.
analysis_steps.pass_criteria: DIG-LIN-CTRL passes and the controller reaches the expected states under regression stimulus.
analysis_steps.debug_focus: baud_tick generation, break counter, PID function, checksum task.

corner_focus.risk: Protocol timing slips if the divider or reset defaults are wrong

tuning_knobs.title: baud_div programming
tuning_knobs.description: First timing knob when sync or frame length looks wrong.

tuning_knobs.title: State transition conditions
tuning_knobs.description: Use when master/slave handoff or break handling does not match the protocol plan.

blocks.name: Control Logic
blocks.role: Sequences bandgap, LDOs, POR release, LIN enable, and clock gating so the control plane respects the analog bring-up order.
blocks.architecture: Counter-based sequencer that asserts enables in a staged order, releases POR late, and disables LIN output activity in sleep mode.

implementation_steps.title: Hold everything low on reset
implementation_steps.description: The reset branch clears every enable and POR output so the chip boots from a known zero state.

implementation_steps.title: Use count thresholds instead of combinational guesses
implementation_steps.description: The sequencer waits for defined count values before turning on each rail or releasing POR.

implementation_steps.title: Gate LIN in sleep
implementation_steps.description: The sleep-mode path explicitly turns off LIN enable even if a previous request was active.

primary_specs.name: Order
primary_specs.reason: Analog dependencies and bus-noise containment.

primary_specs.name: POR release
primary_specs.reason: Digital logic should only run after rails are valid.

primary_specs.name: Sleep behavior
primary_specs.reason: Low-power and safe bus behavior.

sizing_formulas.title: Enable time
sizing_formulas.explanation: The count threshold turns directly into a programmable or architecture-level delay budget.

sizing_formulas.title: POR release point
sizing_formulas.explanation: This is the current RTL condition for releasing the rest of the chip.

dc_operating_points.how_set: Reset branch drives every enable low
dc_operating_points.why: Guarantees deterministic bring-up order.

dc_operating_points.how_set: Counter threshold logic
dc_operating_points.why: Prevents digital logic from running too early.

dc_operating_points.target: follows clk_in unless sleep_mode is asserted
dc_operating_points.how_set: Combinational assignment inside the sequential block
dc_operating_points.why: Avoids toggling logic in sleep mode.

analysis_steps.name: Enable sequencing
analysis_steps.purpose: Verify the staged order, POR release timing, and LIN disable in sleep.
analysis_steps.pass_criteria: DIG-CTRL-SEQUENCE passes and all enables appear in the expected order.
analysis_steps.debug_focus: por_count thresholds and sleep handling.

corner_focus.risk: Enable order breaks if reset or requests arrive unexpectedly

tuning_knobs.title: Threshold counts
tuning_knobs.description: Primary bring-up timing knob.

tuning_knobs.title: Sleep override
tuning_knobs.description: First place to inspect if the bus stays active in low-power mode.
